questions.text: After spending time with people, you typically feel:

options.label: Energized and wanting more social time
Content but ready for some alone time
Drained and need to recharge alone

questions.text: How do you recharge your social battery?

options.label: Mix of social time and solo activities
Quiet alone time with minimal stimulation

questions.text: How much alone time do you need daily?

options.label: Very little - I thrive on social interaction
Most of my day - minimal social interaction

questions.text: After a full day of work/socializing, you prefer to:

options.label: Go out with friends or attend events
Have quiet dinner with close friend/partner
Be alone and do calming activities

questions.text: How do you feel about surprise visitors or calls?

options.label: Excited! Always happy for unexpected connection
Depends on my mood and energy level
Usually not happy - I need advance notice

questions.text: Your ideal weekend includes:

options.label: Balance of social plans and downtime
Mostly alone with one social activity
Complete solitude and no social obligations

questions.text: How quickly does your social battery drain?

options.label: Rarely drains - social time charges me
Gradually - can do several hours before needing break
Relatively quickly - 2-3 hours is my limit
Very quickly - I'm drained within an hour

questions.text: How do you feel about small talk?

options.label: I enjoy it - it's a way to connect
It's fine but I prefer deeper conversation
It drains me - I find it superficial
I actively avoid it - it's exhausting

questions.text: When your battery is low, you:

options.label: Rarely happens - I thrive in social settings
Take a short break then return to socializing
Need several hours alone to recover
Need a full day or more of isolation

questions.text: How do you feel in large groups?

options.label: Okay if I can have smaller conversations

questions.text: Your energy pattern throughout the day is:
How do you recover from a social event that drained you?

options.label: Don't usually feel drained from social events
Quick rest then back to normal
Need several hours of quiet time
Need a full day or more to recover

questions.text: How do you feel about back-to-back social commitments?

options.label: Avoid it - need buffer time between
Never do it - would completely drain me

questions.text: When you're low energy, social interaction feels:
How do you feel about being alone for extended periods?

options.label: Fine for a while, then miss people

questions.text: Your ideal social gathering size is:
How do you prefer to socialize?
How do you feel about phone calls?

options.label: Love them - prefer talking to texting

questions.text: Your stance on last-minute plans:

options.label: Open to it if I have energy
Almost never accept - need mental preparation

questions.text: How many close friends do you need?

options.label: Many - I have a large social circle

questions.text: How do you handle social obligations you don't want to attend?

options.label: Usually go and end up enjoying it
Avoid committing in the first place

questions.text: Your preferred way to spend time with friends:

options.label: Mix of going out and staying in

questions.text: How comfortable are you saying no to social invitations?

options.label: Rarely say no - I love socializing
Very comfortable - I protect my energy
I default to no unless very motivated

questions.text: How do you feel about being the center of attention?

options.label: Love it - I thrive on it
Terrible - I avoid it at all costs

questions.text: Your ideal vacation with friends/partner includes:

options.label: Mix of together time and independent activities
Separate accommodations or solo vacation preferred

questions.text: How do you handle networking or meeting new people?
How many social activities per week feels right?
Your relationship with social media interaction:

options.label: Moderate use - enjoy but need breaks

questions.text: In a relationship, how much together time do you need?

options.label: As much as possible - love being together
A few times a week with space between
Limited time - need lots of independence

questions.text: How do you feel when a partner needs a lot of social time?
How important is it that your partner understands your social battery?

options.label: Essential - deal-breaker if they don't get it

questions.text: When dating, how quickly do you need alone time after dates?

options.label: I don't - could spend days together

questions.text: How do you handle a partner who wants constant communication?

options.label: Love it - I want that too

questions.text: Your ideal living situation with a partner:

options.label: Together all the time - shared space

questions.text: How do you feel about spending holidays with partner's family?
When you need alone time, your partner should:

options.label: I rarely need alone time in relationships
Give me an hour or two
Give me several hours or a day
Understand I need regular extended time alone

questions.text: How do you balance friend time vs partner time?

options.label: Barely manage partner time - little left for friends

questions.text: Your feelings about couples who spend all their time together:
How much do you text/call your partner during the day?

options.label: A few times with meaningful updates

questions.text: Past relationship conflicts often involved:

options.label: Me needing more space than partner liked
Partners feeling neglected by my need for alone time

questions.text: How aware are you of your current social battery level?

options.label: Very aware - I track it actively
Limited awareness - often run on empty

questions.text: How well do you communicate your social needs?

options.label: Hide them to avoid seeming antisocial

questions.text: How do you feel about your social battery needs?

options.label: Wish I needed less alone time
Ashamed or frustrated by my limits

questions.text: How good are you at honoring your social limits?
Do you feel guilty about needing alone time?
How well do others understand your social battery?

options.label: Very well - I've communicated it clearly
Not at all - they think I'm being rude/distant

questions.text: What happens when you ignore your social battery needs?

options.label: Rarely happens - I'm attuned to them
Completely burn out or shut down

questions.text: How do you prevent social burnout?

options.label: Not an issue - socializing energizes me
Carefully manage and limit social commitments

questions.text: How has your awareness of your social battery evolved?

options.label: Learned to understand and manage it
Struggle to understand or accept it

questions.text: How satisfied are you with your social life?

options.label: Very satisfied - it matches my needs
Mostly satisfied with minor adjustments needed

archetypes.name: The Social Energizer
archetypes.description: You gain energy from social interaction and thrive in group settings. Being alone for too long drains you. You're the connector who brings people together and creates vibrant social circles.
characteristics: Feels lonely when alone too long
suggestions: Build in some quiet time to prevent burnout
Practice being comfortable with solitude
Be mindful that others may need more alone time
Ensure social activities are meaningful, not just frequent
Find partners/friends who match your social energy

archetypes.name: The Balanced Ambivert
archetypes.description: You thrive on a healthy balance of social time and solitude. You can adapt to different social situations but need to recharge appropriately. You're comfortable both in groups and alone.
suggestions: Continue honoring your need for balance
Communicate your needs clearly to others
Don't over-schedule social commitments
Check in with yourself regularly about energy levels
Model healthy boundaries for others

archetypes.name: The Selective Socializer
archetypes.description: You enjoy meaningful social connections but in limited doses. Quality over quantity is your motto. You prefer small groups and one-on-one time. Frequent socializing drains you significantly.
suggestions: Honor your social limits without guilt
Choose quality interactions over obligation
Communicate your needs to friends/partners
Build in recovery time after social events
Find friends who respect your boundaries

archetypes.name: The Introverted Recharger
archetypes.description: Social interaction significantly drains your energy, even when you enjoy it. You need substantial alone time to recharge and function well. You're highly sensitive to stimulation and prefer calm, quiet environments.
suggestions: Protect your alone time fiercely
Schedule recovery time after social events
Educate partners/friends about your needs
Choose low-stimulation social activities
Don't apologize for your energy needs
Consider careers/lifestyles that honor your nature

archetypes.name: The Social Anxiety Manager
archetypes.description: Your low social battery may be driven more by anxiety than natural temperament. Social situations trigger stress responses that drain you. With support and strategies, your tolerance could expand.
characteristics: May want more connection but feels blocked
suggestions: Consider therapy for social anxiety
Practice gradual exposure to social situations
Develop coping strategies for anxiety
Distinguish anxiety from natural introversion
Join supportive groups or communities
Be compassionate with yourself during growth

archetypes.name: The Highly Sensitive Hermit
archetypes.description: You're extremely sensitive to social stimulation and prefer extensive solitude. Large amounts of alone time are essential for your wellbeing. You may have just 1-2 close relationships that feel manageable.
characteristics: Deep need for quiet and peace
suggestions: Honor your unique needs without shame
Create a life that supports your nature
Find understanding friends/partners
Set very clear boundaries early
Ensure you have meaningful connection even if limited
Consider whether trauma or other factors need addressing
